name: Goblet Squat
form_cues: Hold the bell close to your chest, elbows tight
Sit between your hips, knees tracking over toes
Brace your core as you stand - exhale on the way up
common_mistake: Letting the knees collapse inward at the bottom of the squat
women_specific_note: Great pre/post-natal squat - load is in front so the lower back stays neutral

name: Barbell Back Squat
form_cues: Screw your feet into the floor to make a stable base
Brace 360 degrees - ribs down, belly hard in all directions
Hip-hinge slightly as you descend, knees track over toes
common_mistake: Caving the chest forward and rounding the upper back as the bar drives you down
women_specific_note: Use a slightly wider stance to suit a wider Q-angle - feet 5-10 degrees out

name: Romanian Deadlift (RDL)
form_cues: Hinge from the hips - push your bum back, do not bend at the spine
Bar stays glued to your legs the whole way down
Stop when you feel a deep hamstring stretch, then drive hips through
common_mistake: Squatting the bar down instead of hinging - knees bend too much, hamstrings never load
women_specific_note: Best lift for posture, glute shape, and protecting your back through perimenopause

name: Conventional Deadlift
form_cues: Set up with the bar over the mid-foot, shins just brushing the bar
Take a big breath, brace, then push the floor away with your feet
Lockout is hips and knees together - squeeze glutes, do not lean back
common_mistake: Lifting hips first so the bar pulls away from the body - turns it into a back lift
women_specific_note: Master the brace before loading heavy - pelvic floor and TVA work together to protect everything

name: Barbell Hip Thrust
form_cues: Shoulder blades on the bench, ribs down, chin tucked
Drive through your heels and squeeze your glutes hard at the top
Lockout hips parallel to the floor - do not overarch your back
common_mistake: Hyperextending the lower back at the top instead of squeezing through the glutes
women_specific_note: The single best lift for glute development - train it 2x/week if that is your goal

name: Bench Press
form_cues: Pin your shoulder blades down and back into the bench
Lower the bar to your sternum with elbows at about 45 degrees from your body
Press up and slightly back so the bar finishes over your shoulders
common_mistake: Flaring elbows wide to 90 degrees - stresses the shoulder and reduces chest engagement
women_specific_note: Most women have proportionally longer arms - a slightly wider grip can feel stronger

name: Overhead Press (OHP)
form_cues: Bar racked on shoulders, elbows under wrists, glutes squeezed
Press straight up - move your head back so the bar travels vertically
Bring your head through under the bar at lockout, biceps next to ears
common_mistake: Pressing the bar in front of the head instead of straight up - costs 30% of strength
women_specific_note: Strong overhead pressing protects bone density in wrist and shoulder through perimenopause

name: Single-Arm Dumbbell Row
form_cues: Brace one hand on the bench, back flat like a tabletop
Pull the dumbbell to your hip, not your chest - elbow drives back
Squeeze the shoulder blade at the top, control the lower
common_mistake: Rotating the torso to hoist the weight - turns it into a side bend, not a row
women_specific_note: Best lift for fixing desk-rounded shoulders and mum-back from carrying babies

name: Lat Pulldown
form_cues: Grip just outside shoulder width, sit tall with a slight lean back
Pull elbows down to your ribs, not behind you
Pause at the bottom, feel the lats squeeze, control the way up
common_mistake: Pulling with the arms and ignoring the lats - bar finishes at the chin and biceps fry first
women_specific_note: Build the lats first - they make pull-ups possible and shape a strong-looking back

name: Farmer Carry
form_cues: Stand tall - chest up, shoulders down and back, eyes forward
Walk in short, controlled steps with feet under your hips
Brace your core like someone is about to nudge you sideways
common_mistake: Letting the shoulders shrug up around the ears - switches off the lats and overloads the traps
women_specific_note: Carries are the most life-relevant strength move - groceries, kids, gym bags feel effortless

name: Front Plank
form_cues: Elbows under shoulders, body in one straight line from head to heels
Tuck the tailbone slightly under to neutralise the lower back
Squeeze glutes and quads, breathe through the brace - no holding your breath
common_mistake: Letting the hips sag toward the floor - turns the plank into a lower-back hang
women_specific_note: Re-train deep core after pregnancy here before any sit-ups or crunches

name: Hollow Hold
form_cues: Press your lower back flat into the floor - no light should get under it
Lift legs and shoulders just enough to keep that flat back
Arms long overhead, breathe shallow into your ribs
common_mistake: Lifting too high so the lower back lifts off the floor and the hip flexors do the work
women_specific_note: Best position to learn TVA engagement - keep the rib-to-pelvis distance closed throughout
